ui.title: CMAPSS FD004 RUL / Failure Prediction GUI
ui.subtitle: Run remaining useful life estimation locally
title.json_input: Enter data as a JSON array
title.missing_policy: Choose how to handle missing data
title.uncertainty_mode: Choose either quantiles or interval levels
title.model_id: Pin execution to a specific model
title.train_algo: Select training algorithm (when set, overrides base_model)
title.header.title: CMAPSS FD004 RUL Workbench
title.header.subtitle: Track engine degradation and remaining life from cycle sequences
step.intro: Goal: split CMAPSS engines by series_id and forecast RUL over cycle steps. Follow: auth → data → run settings → results.
next.step.label: Next: {step}
checklist.data.title: Data checklist
checklist.params.title: Run checklist
section.reference.title: Operations & history
section.support.title: Support & policy
section.support.note: Estimates and help clarify next actions.
section.connection.lead: Confirm API access to unlock the flow.
section.data.lead: Load CMAPSS FD004 engines as separate series and map cycle steps into API timestamps for RUL prediction.
section.params.lead: Define the RUL horizon, cycle frequency, and uncertainty view.
section.results.lead: Run, visualize, and interpret in one place.
section.help.lead: Find the shortest path when you’re stuck.
hint.mode: Use sync for small inputs; jobs for large inputs.
hint.task: Choose RUL forecast, training, backtest, or a drift check. Drift checks require a saved baseline first.
hint.uncertainty_mode: Quantiles and interval levels cannot be used together.
hint.horizon: How many steps ahead to forecast (e.g., daily: 14 = 2 weeks)
hint.frequency: Leave empty to infer. If inference fails, specify it (e.g., 1d, 1h, 15min)
hint.value_unit: Shown on the results axis label only (does not affect inference).
hint.model_id: Pin execution to a specific model. Empty uses default.
hint.train_algo: Empty (auto) uses base_model mapping. Advanced neural options auto-select GPU (MPS/CUDA) when available.
hint.api_base_url: Empty uses same-origin (default). For a different host, enable remote listening on the API side (RULFM_FORECASTING_API_ALLOW_REMOTE=1). Example: https://example.com
hint.api_key_storage: Saved keys expire after 7 days. Unchecked saves only for this session.
hint.json_array: JSON must be an array of records (series_id, timestamp, y)
hint.job_id: Enter an existing job ID to check status.
placeholder.value_unit: e.g., cycles, health index, risk score
billing.points_this_month: Remaining for this run: {remaining}/{limit} forecast points (used {used})
billing.flow_alert_ok: {remaining} forecast points left before the limit
billing.flow_alert_over: Over the limit by {remaining} forecast points
billing.flow_alert_series: With horizon={h}, you can add up to {extra} series
billing.flow_alert_horizon: With series={s}, you can add up to {extra} horizon
billing.lead: Estimated usage and limits based on current input. Final billing follows your plan.
billing.warn_near_limit: Approaching quota. Consider reducing series/horizon or switching to jobs.
billing.error_over_limit_cost01: Quota exceeded. Reduce input size or switch to async jobs.
billing.detail_reduce: Over by {over} points. Reduce series by {reduce_series} (horizon={h}) or horizon by {reduce_horizon} (series={s}).
billing.detail_expand: Capacity left: add up to {extra_series} series (horizon={h}) or {extra_horizon} horizon (series={s}).
billing.usage_local: Usage: estimates only in this UI.
billing.note: Estimate is series x horizon. Actual limits follow your quota policy. COST01/S01 indicate limits exceeded.
billing.ops_note: Limits update automatically with your plan or contract.
models.lead: Sync trained model IDs from the API; memos stay in this browser.
models.embedded_note: Models are part of run settings, so they live here.
status.models_need_key: API key is required to sync models.
results.guide.title: How to read results
results.guide.quantiles: Quantiles: P10 = low, P50 = median, P90 = high
results.guide.intervals: Prediction interval: range based on the specified level
results.train_note: Check model_id for the training result.
results.request_id_note: Required for support inquiries. See Help for steps.
results.details.drift_features_note: Review PSI plus the baseline requested/selected bin count for each feature.
results.legend.title: Legend
results.actions.title: Results actions
results.visual.title: Results visualization
results.summary.title: Run summary
results.summary.integrity_ok: Series {series_id}: chart shows {total} points (matches CSV rows).
results.summary.integrity_mismatch: Series {series_id}: some points are invalid (total {total} / valid {valid}). Check CSV and inputs.
results.evidence.title: Reliability evidence and scope
results.evidence.quality_forecast: Data integrity: {valid}/{total} valid points for the displayed series.
results.evidence.uncertainty_forecast: Uncertainty evidence: quantiles/intervals available on {covered}/{total} points ({coverage}%).
results.evidence.uncertainty_forecast_co: Uncertainty evidence: split conformal (n_residuals={n}, q̂={qhat}, coverage={calib_coverage}%, scaling={scaling}) + interval fields on {covered}/{total} points ({field_coverage}%).
results.evidence.scope_forecast: Scope: interpret within this run setting (series={series}, horizon={horizon}, shown series={series_id}).
results.evidence.limit_no_points: Limitation: no forecast points available, so confidence interpretation is not possible yet. Recheck inputs and run settings.
results.evidence.limit_short_series: Limitation: only {total} points in the shown series, so estimate stability is limited.
results.evidence.limit_low_uncertainty: Limitation: uncertainty coverage is low ({coverage}%), so interval interpretation should be conservative.
results.evidence.limit_assumption: Limitation: intervals/quantiles depend on training distribution and model assumptions; re-validate if distribution shifts.
results.evidence.quality_backtest: Validation evidence: {metrics} metrics, {by_series} by-series rows, {by_horizon} by-horizon rows, and {by_fold} by-fold rows were evaluated.
results.evidence.uncertainty_backtest: Uncertainty evidence: backtest quantifies historical error; it does not guarantee future certainty.
results.evidence.scope_backtest: Scope: valid under folds={folds} and horizon={horizon} settings.
results.evidence.limit_backtest: Limitation: if production data distribution changes, error characteristics can differ from backtest.
results.evidence.uncertainty_train: Uncertainty evidence: training completion alone does not validate interval quality; verify with forecast/backtest.
results.evidence.limit_train: Limitation: successful training is not a quality guarantee; check inference output and error metrics.
results.evidence.uncertainty_drift: Representative feature={feature} baseline bins: requested={requested}, selected={selected}
results.evidence.scope_drift: Candidate scope: records={records}, series={series}. Compared against the stored baseline.
results.evidence.limit_drift: selected_bin_count can differ by feature. Adaptive binning may reduce bins to avoid empty buckets.
results.evidence.exog_currently_ignored: Current algorithms are univariate (y-only); x is currently ignored (not used for forecast).
results.benchmark.title: Benchmark comparison
results.benchmark.summary_leader: Best RMSE in the current table is {model} (RMSE={rmse}).
results.benchmark.summary_empty: No comparable benchmarks are available yet.
results.benchmark.note_snapshot: The benchmark table is a snapshot. Generated at: {generated_at}
results.benchmark.note_run_backtest: Run backtest to compare the current run under the same protocol.
results.benchmark.current_run_note: The current run is shown as the first row.
results.visual.note: Showing series: {series_id}. Data is ordered by timestamp.
results.visual.grid_note: When 0 is within the visible range, the major guideline uses a zero baseline; otherwise a midpoint reference line is shown.
results.visual.note.trend: Trend view: point forecast with band to read direction and uncertainty. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.points: Scatter view: dispersion and outliers, with a trend cue. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.bars: Bars view: compare level per time bucket. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.band: Band view: width reflects uncertainty range. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.area: Area view: magnitude over time with cumulative feel. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.step: Step view: changes emphasized for discrete jumps. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.cumulative: Cumulative view: aggregated total over time. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.change: Change view: period-to-period deltas to read volatility. Series {series_id}.
results.visual.note.index: Index view: normalized to 100 at start for relative comparison. Series {series_id}.
results.interpret.title: How to interpret
results.interpret.trend.caution: Caution: wider bands mean higher uncertainty.
results.interpret.points.when: Use when: checking short-term scatter or anomalies.
results.interpret.points.caution: Caution: sparse points are not conclusive.
results.interpret.bars.intent: Purpose: compare levels across time points.
results.interpret.bars.caution: Caution: long ranges can get crowded.
results.interpret.band.when: Use when: finding where variance is large.
results.interpret.band.caution: Caution: wide bands may require data/model review.
results.interpret.step.caution: Caution: small fluctuations are harder to see.
results.interpret.cumulative.when: Use when: total volume over horizon matters.
results.interpret.cumulative.caution: Caution: mid-horizon slowdowns can be hidden.
results.interpret.index.caution: Caution: small baselines can look exaggerated.
results.series.note: Forecasts are per engine. Target y is RUL, and x contains operating settings plus sensor channels.
results.series.note_exogenous: Without x, the model only sees RUL history; for FD004, sensor channels are usually important.
results.series.note_many: If you have many series, inspect key ones here and validate all via CSV.
results.series.note_model: If model_id is omitted, the default model is used.
backtest.metrics_note: Metric definitions follow the API docs. Lower is generally better.
backtest.by_horizon_note: Errors often grow with longer horizons.
jobs.guide: Jobs are for large inputs. Check status with job_id.
jobs.embedded_note: Jobs are part of result retrieval, so they live here.
error.v02_frequency_infer_failed: Cannot infer time interval. Please specify frequency.
error.v03_quantiles_and_level: Quantiles and level cannot both be set.
error.v04_not_sorted: timestamp is not strictly increasing. Sort records per series.
error.v05_missing_timestamps: Missing timestamps detected. Fill gaps or change missing data handling.
error.cost01_too_large_sync: Input is too large for sync. Use async jobs.
error.s01_payload_too_large: Payload is too large. Reduce input size.
error.title: Run failed
error.suggest.generic: Please review your input. See Help if needed.
error.suggest.a21: Reduce request frequency or wait for the quota window to reset.
error.suggest.v01: Ensure series_id / timestamp / y are present.
error.suggest.v01_torch: For AFNO, x (explanatory features) is optional. Fill series_id / timestamp / y on every row and retry.
error.suggest.v03: Specify either quantiles or level, not both.
error.suggest.v04: Sort timestamps in ascending order per series.
error.suggest.v05: Set missing_policy to ignore or fill missing timestamps.
error.suggest.v06: Provide more history or reduce horizon.
error.suggest.cost01: Reduce input size or run as an async job.
error.suggest.j02: Check job status before fetching the result.
error.suggest.j03: Retry later or open a support ticket with request_id.
error.suggest.m01: Verify model_id or remove it to use the default model.
error.suggest.m02: Wait until the model is ready.
error.suggest.s01: Reduce the number of input records.
warn.large_horizon: Long forecast horizons may reduce accuracy.
warn.input_both: Both CSV and JSON are provided. JSON will be used.
warn.api_base_url_remote:
Caution: connecting to a cross-origin API. Check data you send and CORS settings.
Target: {base_url}
warn.share_link_no_api_key: Shared links/snippets never include API keys. Share keys separately if needed.
warn.share_blocked_sensitive: Sharing was blocked because sensitive-looking data was detected ({path}).
params.link.idle: Data → run settings: ready to sync
params.drift_baseline.title: Drift baseline
params.drift_baseline.note: Save representative healthy data as the baseline first, then switch to candidate data and run the drift check.
params.run_hint: These settings define the run rule. When ready, use the run button in this panel.
info.job_started_large_input: Input is large; running as job (job_id={job_id})
analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.fields: Missing required fields: series_id={series_id}, timestamp={timestamp}, y={y}
analysis.gaps_ack: I reviewed the gaps and want to proceed.
analysis.explore.title: Explore (RUL distribution / engine mix / sensor relationship)
analysis.explore.status_no_forecast: No forecast results yet. Use 'Input' for pre-run analysis, and use forecast analysis after running.
analysis.explore.status_no_residual: No residual evidence yet. Run a forecast first.
analysis.explore.status_insufficient: Not enough usable data to plot (missing / non-numeric values).
analysis.explore.status_no_numeric: No numeric fields found (check y or x.*).
analysis.explore.status_no_category: No categorical fields found (check series_id or x.*).
data.preview.title: Input data map
data.preview.note: First 6 rows are shown. Review the time order per series.
data.preview.explain.no_exog: Split into {series} engine series and model RUL over cycle order (no exogenous inputs).
data.preview.explain.with_exog: Split into {series} engine series and model RUL over cycle order with exogenous inputs: {exog}.
data.guide.title: What gets forecasted (FD004 input meaning)
data.guide.required: Use engine IDs as series_id, map cycle to timestamp, and forecast y=RUL.
data.guide.target: y: remaining useful life. x: operating settings and sensor channels.
data.guide.timestamp: timestamp: cycle mapped into a regular time axis, ascending within each engine.
status.run_blocked_gaps: Gaps detected. Acknowledge the gaps to run.
run.block.data_source_mismatch: No data for the selected input method (data exists in another input)
run.block.input_both: Use either CSV or JSON (not both)
run.block.drift_baseline: Persist or confirm the drift baseline
run.block.cif_disabled: CIFNOcG2 is disabled in this GUI (choose AFNOcG2 or another algorithm)
run.block.torch_data_insufficient: Insufficient AFNO training data (need >= 8 effective samples)
run.block.points_limit: Estimated points exceed the configured limit
status.input_json_priority: Both CSV and JSON are present; JSON will be used.
status.sample_loaded_need_api_key: Sample loaded. Add API key before running.
status.sample_loaded_ready_run: Sample loaded. You can run {action} now.
status.drift_baseline_missing: No drift baseline is stored yet. Save representative data first.
status.drift_baseline_check_failed: Failed to load drift baseline status.
status.drift_baseline_saving: Saving the current input as the drift baseline...
status.drift_baseline_save_failed: Failed to save the drift baseline.
status.train_handoff_ready: Training completed. Switched to forecast and set model_id={model_id}. Press Run forecast.
status.train_handoff_autorun: Training completed. Switched to forecast with model_id={model_id}. Running forecast automatically...
status.auto_forecast_running: Running forecast automatically with the trained model...
status.auto_forecast_done: Training and follow-up forecast both completed.
status.done_no_points: Completed, but no forecast points were returned. Check input window, horizon, and model_id.
status.share_blocked_sensitive: Sharing was blocked due to sensitive data detection
help.lead: Use this section to clarify inputs, results, and safe support handoff.
help.concierge.title: Guided help
help.concierge.body: Select your situation to see the next action and the relevant section.
help.concierge.ask_note: Your input stays in this browser.
help.concierge.label: Select your situation
help.concierge.advice.data:
1) Use CSV or JSON (not both).
2) Ensure series_id/timestamp/y and sorted timestamps.
3) Validate and fix missing/gaps.
help.concierge.advice.run:
1) Set the horizon.
2) Choose quantiles OR level.
3) For V06, review frequency and horizon.
4) Use jobs for large inputs and track job_id.
help.concierge.advice.quota:
1) Check estimate (series x horizon).
2) Reduce series/horizon if near the limit.
3) Switch to jobs when over limit.
help.concierge.advice.results:
1) Use the legend and visual band.
2) Download JSON/CSV.
3) Keep request_id for support.
help.concierge.advice.support:
1) Collect request_id/error_code.
2) Generate support_bundle_public.zip.
3) Send via your support channel.
help.concierge.ask_fallback: No matching guidance. Try JSON error / quota / horizon / request_id.
help.quick.step1: Enter the API key, then check health.
help.quick.step2: Load an FD004 sample or paste preprocessed JSON, then validate.
help.quick.step3: Confirm the horizon/quantiles, run, then inspect the RUL chart and interval width.
help.panel.data_link_body: When data changes, use "Sync from data" to align run settings. Manual edits unlink the sync.
help.panel.first_aid_body: If stuck: input → validate → run. Use error_code and next_action to correct fields or settings.
help.panel.results_body: Use scatter + trend to spot outliers, then switch chart types. Axis labels show time and unit.
help.panel.support_body: Share request_id, error_code, job_id, input size, and a short repro summary.
help.panel.security_body: Keys stay local. Shared links and bundles exclude keys and raw data.
help.panel.quality_body: Gather signals, validate assumptions, and confirm outcomes.
help.flow.input.title: Start here (input & run)
help.flow.input.step1.title: Choose an input format
help.flow.input.step1.body: Use CSV or JSON. Required: series_id/timestamp/y.
help.flow.input.step2.title: Validate and inspect gaps
help.flow.input.step2.body: Run validation, review missing/gaps, and acknowledge if needed.
help.flow.input.step3.title: Align run settings
help.flow.input.step3.body: Confirm inferred frequency, then set horizon and quantiles/level.
help.flow.support.title: Support flow
help.flow.support.step1.title: Collect identifiers
help.flow.support.step1.body: Save request_id, error_code, mode (job/sync), and job_id.
help.flow.support.step2.title: Summarize repro
help.flow.support.step2.body: Reproduce with a sample or summarize size/series/frequency.
help.flow.support.step3.title: Send the bundle
help.flow.support.step3.body: Attach support_bundle_public.zip with a short summary.
help.flow.dev.title: Developer flow
help.flow.dev.step1.title: Confirm API contract
help.flow.dev.step1.body: Use Swagger to review required fields, types, and errors.
help.flow.dev.step2.title: Design the call path
help.flow.dev.step2.body: Choose sync vs job. COST01 should switch to jobs.
help.flow.dev.step3.title: Operate & monitor
help.flow.dev.step3.body: Track job_id and store request_id. Group errors by i18n key.
help.note.contact: There is no support input field here. Contact support separately with request_id and the bundle.
help.bullet.format: Confirm required fields and timestamp order.
help.bullet.sample: If unsure: load a similar sample; it syncs run settings.
billing.detail_pending: Consumption appears once data size and horizon are set.
help.bullet.validate: Validate before run to populate analysis and catch V01/V04/V05.
help.bullet.json_or_csv: Use CSV or JSON, not both (JSON takes priority).
help.bullet.frequency: Specify frequency if inference fails (V02) or data has gaps.
help.bullet.quantiles: Choose quantiles OR level (V03), not both.
help.bullet.min_history: For stability, min_history=max(20, 2×seasonal period, 2×horizon). Frequency determines seasonal period.
help.bullet.results_chart: After run: choose a chart type and confirm ranges in highlights.
help.bullet.jobs: Large inputs: use jobs and track job_id/progress.
help.trouble.v01: V01: Missing required fields → check series_id / timestamp / y.
help.trouble.v02: V02: Frequency not inferred → specify frequency (e.g., 1d, 1h).
help.trouble.v03: V03: quantiles and level both set → choose one.
help.trouble.v04: V04: timestamp not strictly increasing → sort by series_id, then timestamp.
help.trouble.v06: V06: insufficient history → add more data to meet min_history.
help.trouble.m01: M01: model_id missing → train first (/v1/train), then set model_id.
help.trouble.cost01: COST01: too large for sync → run via /v1/jobs.
help.trouble.s01: S01: payload too large → split/reduce input.
help.dev.api_relative: Base URL: same host + /v1/ (override via proxy if needed).
help.dev.endpoints: Sync: POST /v1/forecast. Jobs: POST /v1/jobs -> GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} -> GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}/result.
help.dev.jobs: Use jobs for large inputs or COST01; poll until status=succeeded.
help.dev.examples: Examples: use API docs or copy snippets from this GUI.
client.err.data_required: CSV file or JSON input is required
client.err.horizon_invalid: Forecast horizon must be an integer >= 1
client.err.training_hours_invalid: Training time must be a number >= 0.05
client.err.job_poll_timeout: Timed out while waiting for job result (job_id={job_id}, attempts={total}). Refresh jobs and fetch result again.
confirm.run_cost: This run is estimated at {points} forecast points. Remaining after run: {remaining}/{limit}. Continue?
